summary_en: Final dividend for FY3/26 (year ending June 2026) raised from ¥240/share to ¥350/share (+¥110, 46% increase). Full-year dividend now ¥350/share vs. prior forecast of ¥240/share. Payout ratio targeted at 40%. Revision driven by Q3 outperformance in utilities, semiconductors, telecoms, and acceleration of mobility projects.
title_en: Notice of Revisions to Full-Year Business Performance Forecast and Dividend Forecast (Dividend Increase) for FY3/26
translation_en_brief: Nishikawa Keisoku announced on 14 May 2026 a revision to its full-year earnings and dividend forecasts for the fiscal year ending June 30, 2026. Full-year net profit forecast was raised from ¥2,000 million to ¥3,000 million (50% increase), with operating profit lifted from ¥2,800 million to ¥4,100 million. The company's final dividend forecast was increased from ¥240/share to ¥350/share, a raise of ¥110/share (46%). The company pays no interim dividend, so the full-year payout is ¥350/share, compared to ¥320/share in the prior year. The revision reflects strong Q3 results across utilities, semiconductors, telecoms, and the acceleration of large mobility projects originally scheduled for the next fiscal year. The company targets an annual payout ratio of approximately 40% while maintaining dividend stability based on return on equity, even if temporary business deterioration occurs.
